home.heroBody: Science shows eating 30 different plants a week boosts gut diversity. Project Food makes it simple to track yours.
home.feature1Body: Fruits, vegetables, herbs, nuts, seeds, legumes, whole grains — every plant counts.
home.feature2Body: See your progress day by day and build a habit that sticks.
home.feature3Body: Hit 30 plants and unlock AI-powered suggestions for next week's shopping list.

placeholder.workInProgress: This page is being built. Check back soon.

about.heroSubtitle: A story about food, family, and thirty plants a week.
about.story1: My grandfather had a grocery store in Tongelre, Eindhoven. Before the store, he had a horse and cart, riding through the neighbourhood, selling fruits and vegetables door to door. Eventually the cart became a shop. My mother grew up working there. My uncles did too. By the time my uncle took over and moved to a busier street, I had just been born. There is a photo of me, days old, lying between the fruit on the counter.
about.story2: Growing up, I was always around. Piling empty crates, loading the van for wholesale runs, learning which tomatoes were ripe. As I got older I started cooking, preparing dishes to sell in the store, and somewhere along the way that turned into a genuine love. I still cook every day, still experiment, still taste everything.
about.discovery1: About two years ago, my girlfriend and I watched a gut health documentary on Netflix. The message was surprising: it's not about eating the right foods more often, it's about eating a wide variety of plants. As many different ones as possible.
about.discovery2: The science made sense. Thousands of years ago we ate whatever we could find, roots, berries, seeds, leaves, everything the land offered. That variety fed an entire ecosystem of bacteria in the gut. Different bacteria for different plants. Together, they create balance. Modern diets, heavy on processed food, bread, meat, and sugar, have narrowed that variety dramatically. Researchers now suggest a simple target: 30 different plants per week. Fruits, vegetables, grains, legumes, nuts, seeds, herbs, and spices all count.
about.callout: 30 plants a week. Backed by science, made into a game.
about.appTitle: It should be easier. And more fun.
about.app1: We took the challenge seriously. Two years later, I was still tracking plants in a notes app and on scraps of paper, and I thought: there should be a better way. What if there was an app that tracked your variety for you, told you which plants you were missing, and gave you inspiration for next week's shopping? What if you could challenge friends and family to eat more diverse, and actually make a game of it?
about.vision1: Project Food isn't about eating perfectly. It's about eating curiously, exploring vegetables you've never tried, adding a new herb to your list, building a habit that makes you feel genuinely good. Whether you grew up between the vegetables or you're just discovering them, there's always more to explore.

contact.title: Come say hello.
contact.subtitle: We hang out on Discord, join the community.
contact.body: Ask questions, share what you're eating, suggest new plants, or just follow along. It's the best place to connect with other Project Food users and give feedback directly to the team.

form.success: Message sent! We'll get back to you soon.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using Project Food, you agree to these Terms of Service. Please read them carefully.
terms.s1Body: Project Food is a plant diversity tracker. It helps you log and track the variety of plants you eat each week, with a goal of 30 different plants. Project Food is not a medical service and does not provide medical, dietary, or nutritional advice.
terms.s2Body: You must be at least 16 years old to use Project Food. If you are between 13 and 16, you may only use the service with the consent of a parent or legal guardian. By creating an account, you confirm that you meet this requirement.
terms.s3Body: You are responsible for keeping your account credentials secure. Your account is personal and may not be shared or transferred. We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms.
terms.s4Intro: You agree not to use Project Food to:
terms.s4Item2: scrape, copy, or redistribute any data from the platform
terms.s4Item4: use the service in any way that violates applicable laws
terms.s5Body: Project Food provides personal tracking tools only. Nothing on this platform should be interpreted as medical, dietary, or nutritional advice. Always consult a qualified health professional for advice about your diet or health.
terms.s6Body: The Project Food app, website, and all original content are owned by Project Food. Your plant log data remains yours. By using the service, you grant Project Food a limited licence to store and display your data to provide the service.
terms.s7Body: To the extent permitted by Dutch law, Project Food is not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service. The service is provided as-is, without warranties of any kind.
terms.s8Body: You may delete your account at any time via the Account settings. We may suspend or terminate your access if you violate these Terms. Upon termination, your data will be deleted in accordance with our Privacy Policy.
terms.s9Body: These Terms are governed by Dutch law. Any disputes arising from these Terms will be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Netherlands. The Dutch language version of these Terms prevails in case of discrepancy.
terms.s10Body: Questions about these Terms? Join us on our Discord community.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Project Food collects, uses, and protects your personal data. It applies to all users of the Project Food app and website.
privacy.s1Body: Project Food is the data controller responsible for your personal data. You can reach us via our Discord community.
privacy.s2Intro: When you use Project Food, we collect the following:
privacy.s2Item1: Name and email address, provided by Google when you sign in via Google OAuth
privacy.s2Item2: Your plant logs — the plants you log and when
privacy.s2Item3: Your locale preference, stored in a functional cookie called pf_locale
privacy.s2Item4: Standard technical logs generated by our hosting and database providers
privacy.s3Intro: We process your data on the following grounds under GDPR Article 6:
privacy.s3Item1: Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) — to provide the service you signed up for
privacy.s3Item2: Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) — to maintain security and stability of the platform
privacy.s4Intro: We use the following trusted sub-processors to operate the service:
privacy.s4Item1: Supabase — database and authentication (EU/US, Data Processing Agreement in place)
privacy.s4Item2: Vercel — hosting and edge network (US, Standard Contractual Clauses in place)
privacy.s5Body: Project Food uses one cookie: pf_locale, which stores your language preference for up to one year. This is a strictly functional cookie — it does not track you and does not require your consent under GDPR. If we add analytics or advertising cookies in the future, we will update this policy and request your consent.
privacy.s6Intro: Under GDPR Articles 15–20, you have the right to:
privacy.s6Item1: Access — request a copy of the data we hold about you (Art. 15)
privacy.s6Item2: Rectification — correct inaccurate or incomplete data (Art. 16)
privacy.s6Item3: Erasure — request deletion of your account and all associated data (Art. 17)
privacy.s6Item4: Portability — receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format (Art. 20)
privacy.s6Item5: Restriction and objection — limit or object to how we process your data (Art. 18, 21)
privacy.s6Contact: To exercise any of these rights, reach us on our Discord community.
privacy.s7Body: We keep your data for as long as your account is active. After you request account deletion, your personal data is permanently deleted within 30 days.
privacy.s8Body: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data to any third party, ever.
privacy.s9Body: If you believe we have not handled your data correctly, you have the right to file a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) at www.autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl.
privacy.s10Body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. If we make significant changes, we will notify you by email before they take effect.
privacy.s11Body: For privacy-related questions, join our Discord community.
